methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $4.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.1B - $9.2B.

data.narrative:
Chaos Industries, a Series D defense and security company founded by John Tenet and Gordon Kraft, carries a current post-money valuation of $4.5B following $1B+ in cumulative fundraising. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities including Saronic, Epirus, Onebrief, Anduril Industries, and Flock Safety.

Two valuation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D progression, yielding $6.8B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Saronic ($9.2B), Epirus ($1.4B), and Onebrief ($1.1B)—produces a median of $1.4B. The peer range spans $1.1B to $9.2B, reflecting material heterogeneity in company scale and profile within the cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $7.8B (high), with a median of $4.0B. The current $4.5B valuation sits slightly above the median, supported by the stage-progression method but substantially above peer-median benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations—a 8.4x spread between min and max—and the limited peer set of three comparable companies, both of which constrain confidence in single-point estimates. The peer-median method's applicability depends on the assumption that Saronic, Epirus, and Onebrief are sufficiently comparable on revenue scale, growth trajectory, and margin profile.
